since_last: 71% of 86 AI names green; AI Chips & Compute leads +6.79%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: Markets closed — Juneteenth
cards.body: US equity markets are shut for Juneteenth, so the tape is frozen at the previous session's close. Everything below reflects that last close — not live trading — and refreshes when the market reopens.

cards.headline: Last close: a broad bid, 71% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +1.08% · BTC +0.57% · Gold -1.46% · Nasdaq100 +2.48%. At the last close, 71% of 86 AI names were green — a broad bid. AI Chips & Compute did the heavy lifting at +6.79%.

cards.headline: AI Chips & Compute leads, AI Applications & Data Software lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is AI Chips & Compute at +6.79%; weakest is AI Applications & Data Software at -0.70%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: CEVA is the standout at +10.71%
cards.body: Leaders: CEVA +10.71%, INTC +10.64%, SMCI +10.37%, LSCC +9.91%, KLAC +8.73%. Laggards: SPCX -9.26%, INOD -6.85%, ADBE -5.33%, IBM -5.05%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -9.26% at $191.82
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -9.26% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
